title: Edgar Morin: 'I doubt humanity even as I believe in it'
subhead: Over a series of conversations held between 2024 and 2026 with Le Monde, the philosopher and WWII resistant analyzed the contemporary world's upheaval, from the rise of authoritarianism in the West to conflicts in the Middle East, as well as the impact of new technologies and environmental issues.

title: Trump evacuated after shots fired at White House Correspondents dinner
Internet access in Iran becomes a privilege granted by the government
Israeli strikes kill six in south Lebanon despite extended ceasefire, fresh raids reported
subhead: A new wave of bombardments has been reported overnight, even as a recently extended ceasefire with Hezbollah appeared to fray amid mutual accusations of violations.

title: Trump cancels envoys' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks
subhead: Trump said the cancellation of the trip did not mean the United States would resume fighting with Iran.

title: 'It is possible to do without synthetic fertilizers,' says researcher Fabien Esculier
subhead: The engineer highlights the tremendous fertilizing potential of human urine and feces at a time of geopolitical and environmental crises. This potential remains completely untapped, despite having been used for over 2,000 years.

title: Annette Messager: 'Without art, I would have become a very mean woman'
subhead: 'I wouldn't be where I am today if...' In this series, Le Monde interviews a public figure about a decisive moment in their life.  The internationally renowned visual artist recalls the strange atmosphere of the seaside town of Berck, where she grew up.

How Iranian propaganda lures the West into distraction
subhead: Pro-Iranian accounts are flooding the internet with AI-generated satirical videos that exploit Lego's popularity and Donald Trump's unpopularity, in an attempt to divert Western attention from the suffering of the Iranian people.

title: 'How can anyone love Céline Dion? The hysteria surrounding her Paris concerts is astonishing'
subhead: After the spectacular sale of nearly 500,000 tickets for a series of shows in Paris this autumn, Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin questions in his column what drives people to love (or hate) the singer.

title: Sealing the internet: How Putin wants to cut Russia off from the rest of the world
subhead: Controlling information has become a matter of survival for the Russian leader. Since 2019, he has sought to create a 'sovereign RuNet' under his control and cut off from the world. The technical implementation of this project has accelerated since the start of the war in Ukraine in 2022.

title: French Socialists' policy platform: Neither new nor complete, but at least it exists
